title: aspidium groenlandicum
intro: Aspidium groenlandicum, also known as Greenland spleenwort, is a species of fern native to the Arctic tundra. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of tannins and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of A. groenlandicum have been reported to provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. Herbal preparations of A. groenlandicum can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for topical or internal use, but its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
